title: conyza rhizocephala
intro: Conyza rhizocephala, also known as the Horseweed or Mexican tea plant, is a perennial herb native to the Americas and a member of the daisy family. Conyza rhizocephala has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties as well. Conyza rhizocephala can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, or as a tincture by soaking the plant in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin.
